metadata.title: Grade 1
domains: Number and Operations in Base Ten
metadata.notes: Auto-ingested from Common Standards Project (set 67810E9EF6944F9383DCC602A3484C23_D10003FB_grade-01). Node structure matches KnowledgeNode in knowledge_zome/integrity/src/lib.rs.

nodes.title: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the...
nodes.description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, fourths, and quarters, and use the phrases half of, fourth of, and quarter of. Describe the whole as two of, or four of the shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.

academic_standards.description: Partition circles and rectangles into two and four equal shares, describe the shares using the words halves, fourths, and quarters, and use the phrases half of, fourth of, and quarter of. Describe the whole as two of, or four of the shares. Understand for these examples that decomposing into more equal shares creates smaller shares.

nodes.title: Compose two-dimensional shapes (rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, h...
nodes.description: Compose two-dimensional shapes (rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles, and quarter-circles) or three-dimensional shapes (cubes, right rectangular prisms, right circular cones, and right circular cylinders) to create a composite shape, and compose new shapes from the composite shape.

academic_standards.description: Compose two-dimensional shapes (rectangles, squares, trapezoids, triangles, half-circles, and quarter-circles) or three-dimensional shapes (cubes, right rectangular prisms, right circular cones, and right circular cylinders) to create a composite shape, and compose new shapes from the composite shape.

nodes.title: Distinguish between defining attributes (e
nodes.description: Distinguish between defining attributes (e.g., triangles are closed and three-sided) versus non-defining attributes (e.g., color, orientation, overall size); build and draw shapes to possess defining attributes.

academic_standards.description: Distinguish between defining attributes (e.g., triangles are closed and three-sided) versus non-defining attributes (e.g., color, orientation, overall size); build and draw shapes to possess defining attributes.

nodes.title: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories
nodes.description: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories; ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and how many more or less are in one category than in another.

academic_standards.description: Organize, represent, and interpret data with up to three categories; ask and answer questions about the total number of data points, how many in each category, and how many more or less are in one category than in another.

nodes.title: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks
nodes.description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.

academic_standards.description: Tell and write time in hours and half-hours using analog and digital clocks.

nodes.title: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units, by laying ...
nodes.description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units, by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end; understand that the length measurement of an object is the number of same-size length units that span it with no gaps or overlaps.

academic_standards.description: Express the length of an object as a whole number of length units, by laying multiple copies of a shorter object (the length unit) end to end; understand that the length measurement of an object is the number of same-size length units that span it with no gaps or overlaps.

nodes.title: Order three objects by length
nodes.description: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.

academic_standards.description: Order three objects by length; compare the lengths of two objects indirectly by using a third object.

nodes.title: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range...
nodes.description: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 (positive or zero differences), using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.
nodes.subdomain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

academic_standards.description: Subtract multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 from multiples of 10 in the range 10-90 (positive or zero differences), using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used.

nodes.title: Given a two-digit number, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than the number, w...
nodes.description: Given a two-digit number, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than the number, without having to count; explain the reasoning used.
nodes.subdomain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

academic_standards.description: Given a two-digit number, mentally find 10 more or 10 less than the number, without having to count; explain the reasoning used.

nodes.title: Add within 100, including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number, a...
nodes.description: Add within 100, including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number, and adding a two-digit number and a multiple of 10, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used. Understand that in adding two-digit numbers, one adds tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.
nodes.subdomain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

academic_standards.description: Add within 100, including adding a two-digit number and a one-digit number, and adding a two-digit number and a multiple of 10, using concrete models or drawings and strategies based on place value, properties of operations, and/or the relationship between addition and subtraction; relate the strategy to a written method and explain the reasoning used. Understand that in adding two-digit numbers, one adds tens and tens, ones and ones; and sometimes it is necessary to compose a ten.

nodes.title: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, ...
nodes.description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.
nodes.subdomain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

academic_standards.description: Compare two two-digit numbers based on meanings of the tens and ones digits, recording the results of comparisons with the symbols >, =, and <.

nodes.title: Count to 120, starting at any number less than 120
nodes.description: Count to 120, starting at any number less than 120. In this range, read and write numerals and represent a number of objects with a written numeral.
nodes.subdomain: Number and Operations in Base Ten

academic_standards.description: Count to 120, starting at any number less than 120. In this range, read and write numerals and represent a number of objects with a written numeral.

nodes.title: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation rel...
nodes.description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers.

academic_standards.description: Determine the unknown whole number in an addition or subtraction equation relating three whole numbers.

nodes.title: Understand the meaning of the equal sign, and determine if equations involvin...
nodes.description: Understand the meaning of the equal sign, and determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.

academic_standards.description: Understand the meaning of the equal sign, and determine if equations involving addition and subtraction are true or false.

nodes.title: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtractio...
nodes.description: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10. Use strategies such as counting on; making ten (e.g., 8 + 6 = 8 + 2 + 4 = 10 + 4 = 14); decomposing a number leading to a ten (e.g., 13 - 4 = 13 - 3 - 1 = 10 - 1 = 9); using the relationship between addition and subtraction (e.g., knowing that 8 + 4 = 12, one knows 12 - 8 = 4); and creating equivalent but easier or known sums (e.g., adding 6 + 7 by creating the known equivalent 6 + 6 + 1 = 12 + 1 = 13).

academic_standards.description: Add and subtract within 20, demonstrating fluency for addition and subtraction within 10. Use strategies such as counting on; making ten (e.g., 8 + 6 = 8 + 2 + 4 = 10 + 4 = 14); decomposing a number leading to a ten (e.g., 13 - 4 = 13 - 3 - 1 = 10 - 1 = 9); using the relationship between addition and subtraction (e.g., knowing that 8 + 4 = 12, one knows 12 - 8 = 4); and creating equivalent but easier or known sums (e.g., adding 6 + 7 by creating the known equivalent 6 + 6 + 1 = 12 + 1 = 13).

nodes.title: Relate counting to addition and subtraction (e
nodes.description: Relate counting to addition and subtraction (e.g., by counting on 2 to add 2).

academic_standards.description: Relate counting to addition and subtraction (e.g., by counting on 2 to add 2).

nodes.description: Apply properties of operations as strategies to add and subtract.

nodes.title: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum i...
nodes.description: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

academic_standards.description: Solve word problems that call for addition of three whole numbers whose sum is less than or equal to 20, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

nodes.title: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve word problems involving situa...
nodes.description: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.

academic_standards.description: Use addition and subtraction within 20 to solve word problems involving situations of adding to, taking from, putting together, taking apart, and comparing, with unknowns in all positions, e.g., by using objects, drawings, and equations with a symbol for the unknown number to represent the problem.
